name: LBBB — Left Bundle Branch Block
identifyBy: Broad notched or slurred R wave in I, aVL, V5–V6 ('M' pattern in lateral leads)
Absent septal Q waves in I, aVL, V5–V6 (loss of normal septal activation)
Deep broad S wave in V1 (rS or QS pattern)
Secondary ST and T wave changes: discordant — opposite direction to QRS

measurements.stSegment: Discordant — depression/inversion in leads with tall R; elevation in leads with S/QS

management: LBBB itself does not require treatment — investigate the cause
New LBBB with chest pain: treat as STEMI equivalent (Sgarbossa criteria)
Causes: ischaemic heart disease, hypertension, aortic stenosis, cardiomyopathy, idiopathic (Lenegre's disease)
Consider cardiac resynchronisation therapy (CRT) in heart failure with LBBB and EF <35%
Bundle of His pacing: emerging therapy for LBBB-mediated dyssynchrony
pearls: New LBBB + chest pain = STEMI equivalent — activate cath lab if Sgarbossa criteria met
LBBB masks ischaemia — ST changes in LBBB are expected (discordant); only concordant changes are diagnostic
William (W) pattern in V1–V6: WiLLiaM = W in V1, M in V6 (LBBB); MaRRoW = M in V1, W in V6 (RBBB)
Septal Q waves are abolished in LBBB — their presence suggests co-existing anterior infarction
LBBB with rate-related (tachycardia-dependent) or bradycardia-dependent variants exist
differentials: RBBB (M pattern in V1, W in V6 — opposite)
Ventricular paced rhythm (pacing spike before wide QRS)
Left anterior fascicular block (narrow QRS, left axis only)
